assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lists 7 supported versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) all marked 'NO' in the Affected? column. Each ships serde_json >= 1.0.137 (versions 1.0.137, 1.0.138, or 1.0.139), all outside the CVE affected range of < 1.0.135. The summary states: 'NO supported versions are affected.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 7 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md explicitly states 'Decision: Case C -- No Supported Versions Affected' and 'Recommendation: Close as Not a Bug (not affected).' Proposed Jira action #2 reads: 'Transition TC-8002 to Closed with resolution Not a Bug.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md proposed Jira action #1 includes a detailed comment with a table listing all 7 versions and their serde_json versions (e.g., '2.1.0 | 1.0.137 | NO', '2.2.0 | 1.0.138 | NO', etc.) plus the summary: 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137 which is outside the affected range (< 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 7 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md proposed Jira action #3 states: 'Set VEX Justification (customfield_12345) to Component not Present.' The VEX Justification section reiterates 'Value: Component not Present' with rationale: 'The vulnerable package version (serde_json < 1.0.135) is not shipped in any supported product version.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 7 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md under 'Remediation Tasks' explicitly states: 'None. No remediation tasks are created because no supported versions are affected (Case C).' No upstream backport or downstream propagation tasks appear anywhere in the outputs.
